articles.description:
Dairy Queen welcomes its new Swicy Menu, featuring sweet-and-spicy flavors.

Available at participating DQ Grill & Chill restaurants nationwide, the menu includes;
- FlameThrower Chicken Strip Basket - Four white-meat, crispy, seasoned chicken strips coated in a blend of spicy peppers and smoky seasonings inspired by the chain's Flamethrower sauce, served with vanilla soft serve for dipping, fries, Texas toast, and a choice of dipping sauce. It's also available with six chicken strips instead of four.
- Spicy Chicken Strip Sandwich - Two crispy chicken strips, tomatoes, chopped lettuce, and Flamethrower sauce, served on a toasted bun.
- New Pineapple Lemonade DQ Cooler - Lemonade and pineapple syrup blended with soft serve and Misty Slush, then topped with Tajin chili-lime seasoning.
- New Tajin and Pineapple Lemonade DQ Sparkler with Tajin - Sparkling lemonade mixed with pineapple syrup and topped with Tajin seasoning.
Photo via Dairy Queen.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Dairy Queen Rolls Out New Swicy Menu

articles.description:
New Nissin Cup Noodles Protein is now available in select grocery retailers nationwide. The instant ramen features 16 grams of protein (more than twice the 6 to 7 grams of protein you find in regular Cup Noodles) in every cup.

According to the ingredients, the new line includes soy protein isolate and bone broth for that extra protein.
Cup Noodles Protein is available in three flavors: Rich & Savory Beef, Rich & Savory Chicken, and Hot & Spicy Chicken.
As with standard Cup Noodles, you prepare Cup Noodles Protein by adding hot water and waiting for three minutes.
The new high-protein version of Cup Noodles can be found online (including at Amazon in a pack of six [affiliate disclosure - I may earn a commission if you buy something through this link]) and at select retailers nationwide, including Albertsons, Kroger, and Walmart, for a suggested price of $1.33 per cup.
Nissin also recently introduced three limited-time, chicken wing-inspired instant ramen flavors: Spicy Buffalo with Ranch, Lemon Pepper, and Garlic Parmesan. The three flavors are available now exclusively at Walmart for $1.18 per cup.
Photo via Nissin Foods.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: New Cup Noodles Protein Arrives in Stores

articles.description:
Calling it the chain's "biggest and boldest burger yet," McDonald's Big Arch Burger will arrive at participating locations nationwide starting March 3, 2026, for a limited time.

The Big Arch features two quarter-pound beef patties, three slices of melted white cheddar cheese, both crispy and slivered onions, lettuce, pickles, and Big Arch Sauce, served on a toasted sesame-and-poppy-seeded bun.
The key highlight of the burger is the new Big Arch Sauce, which McDonald's describes as "a tangy, creamy sauce with a perfect balance of mustard, pickle, and sweet tomato flavors.
While it's not yet March 3, 2026, the Big Arch is already available at some locations, including my local McDonald's, where it costs $8.59 (versus $7.29 for a Double Quarter Pounder with Cheese). That makes it the priciest burger on the menu, although the Double Bacon Quarter Pounder with Cheese comes in a close second at $8.49.
The Big Arch comes in at 1020 calories (versus 740 calories for the Double Quarter Pounder with Cheese and 850 for the Double Bacon Quarter Pounder with Cheese), which is the most calories in McDonald's burger lineup. The new sauce, crispy onions, and an extra slice of cheese appear to make up the difference.
Photo via McDonald's.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: McDonald's Big Arch Coming March 3, 2026

articles.description:
The $9.99 "Any Pizza. Any Toppings" deal is back for a limited time at Domino's through April 6, 2026.

An online-only deal, the "Any Pizza. Any Toppings" deal lets you get any pizza on the Domino's menu in any size (except for XL and Specialty Pizzas) for $9.99 each (price may vary; it costs $10.99 near me in Los Angeles).
As far as the toppings go, you can choose up to seven except for on the Pan and NY Style crusts, which are limited to six toppings. You can get the pizza with Parmesan Stuffed Crust, but it will cost extra ($3 more near me).
Certain crusts are only available in select sizes. For example, Domino's Handmade Pan pizza and Parmesan Stuffed Crust pizza are only available in a 12-inch medium size, while the NY Style pizza is not available in a small.
Photo via Domino's.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Domino's Offers $9.99 "Any Pizza. Any Toppings." Deal Through April 6, 2026

articles.description:
Starting at noon ET on February 24, 2026, Denny's will be selling a limited number of Spring Break Pass fanny packs good for a free Everyday Value Slam every day throughout the 2026 spring break season (from March 2 to April 14, 2026).

The Denny's Spring Break Pass crossbody bag is priced at $14.99 and will be sold from the Denny's Diner Drip online store. Denny's Rewards members can access the sale an hour early at 11AM ET.
The Everyday Value Slam includes two eggs, two applewood-smoked bacon strips or pork sausage links, plus either two buttermilk pancakes, two brioche French toast slices, or a biscuit and gravy. Prices vary, but it costs $6.99 near me in Los Angeles.
If you manage to purchase one of the fanny packs, the free Everyday Value Slam can be redeemed once a day when dining in at a participating Denny's restaurant.
Photo via Denny's.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Denny's Selling Fanny Pack Good for Free Everyday Value Slams During Spring Break Season

articles.description:
The Twister wrap is back on the menu at participating KFC restaurants nationwide.

It's been a while since KFC last offered the Twister, but the fried chicken chain tested its return in 2024. This time around, you can get a Classic Twister or a new Spicy Twister, with or without bacon.
The Classic Twister features two Original Recipe chicken tenders, lettuce, diced tomato, and Pepper Mayo, wrapped in a warm flour tortilla. The Spicy Twister is much the same but swaps out Pepper Mayo for spicy Zinger Sauce. Opting for bacon costs extra and nets you two slices of hickory-smoked bacon.
Prices vary, but both the Classic Twister and Spicy Twister cost $4.99 near me in Los Angeles. Adding bacon tacks on $2.
KFC also continues to offer its Nashville Hot sauce, which returned last month and is available on the fast-food chain's fried chicken, tenders, and chicken sandwiches.
Finally, KFC adds a limited-time $20 8-piece Build-A-Bucket deal. For the price, you get eight Original Recipe chicken tenders or dark meat fried chicken, plus a choice of four individual sides, four dipping sauces, and four buttermilk biscuits.
Photo via KFC.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: KFC Brings Back the Twister (2026)

Jack in the Box's Matcha Iced Latte (pictured) features matcha (Japanese green tea) swirled with sweetened cream and vanilla, served over ice.
The Oreo Matcha Shake is a vanilla shake mixed with matcha and Oreo cookie crumbles, crowned with whipped topping.
Prices vary by location, but a regular Oreo Matcha Shake costs $5.29 ($5.79 for a large) near me here in Los Angeles, while the Matcha Iced Latte starts at $3.49 for a small ($3.99 for a large).
Both drinks also happen to be green, which fits the timing with St. Patrick's Day coming up.
Photo via Jack in the Box.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Jack in the Box Introduces New Matcha Iced Latte and Oreo Matcha Shake

articles.description:
Jack in the Box welcomes a discounted meal deal close to the $5 sweet spot with the introduction of the new $4.99 Munch Better Deal Meal.

For the price, you get a choice of either a Jr. Jumbo Jack Cheeseburger, a Jr. Chicken Sandwich, a Double Jr. Jumbo Jack (for an extra $1.50), or a Really Big Chicken Sandwich (also for an extra $1.50), plus small fries and a drink.
In case you're wondering what you get with each burger/sandwich, here are the details:


Jr. Jumbo Jack Cheeseburger - A grilled small beef patty, American cheese, tomato, lettuce, pickle, onions, ketchup, and mayo, served on a toasted bun.
Jr. Chicken Sandwich - A crispy-fried chicken patty, lettuce, and mayo, served on a toasted bun.
Double Jr. Jumbo Jack Cheeseburger - Two grilled small beef patties, two slices of American cheese, tomatoes, lettuce, pickles, onions, ketchup, and mayo, served on a toasted bun.
Really Big Chicken Sandwich - Two crispy-fried chicken patties, two slices of Swiss cheese, lettuce, tomato, and mayo, served on a toasted bun.


The name is an interesting choice as it's the same name that Jack in the Box used for a previous meal deal promotion late last year. That offer included the Gremlins Midnight Meal, the $7 Brunchie Meal, and the $10 Lunchie Meal.
As an alternative, the fast-food chain also offers the $6 Jack Pack (price may vary) that includes a Jr. Jumbo Jack Cheeseburger, a Jr. Chicken Sandwich, a taco, small seasoned curly fries, and a small drink.
Photo via Jack in the Box.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Jack in the Box Puts Together $4.99 Munch Better Deal Meal

articles.description:
The Thin Mints Frosty is back on the menu at Wendy's and is now called the "Thin Mints Frosty Swirl." Joining it is the new Thin Mints Frosty Fusion.

The Thin Mints Frosty Swirl (you can find my review of it here) features a Vanilla Frosty swirled and topped with a minty cookie crumble sauce inspired by Girl Scouts Thin Mints cookies.
The new Thin Mints Frosty Fusion (pictured) features a Vanilla Frosty mixed with a minty cookie crumble sauce and crushed Girl Scout Thin Mints cookies.
Both desserts can also be made with a Chocolate Frosty instead of Vanilla and are available now for a limited time.
Prices vary, but at a Wendy's here in Los Angeles, the Thin Mints Frosty Swirl ranges from $2.99 to $3.99, while the Thin Mints Frosty Fusion ranges from $4.19 to $5.49.
Additionally, Girl Scouts will be selling cookies at participating Wendy's restaurants nationwide during National Girl Scout Cookie Weekend from February 20-22, and throughout the Girl Scout Cookie season. 
Photo via Wendy's.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Wendy's Debuts New Thin Mints Frosty Fusion and Brings Back Thin Mints Frosty

articles.description:
The Maple Bourbon BBQ Whopper is back for a limited time at Burger King.

The Whopper variant was introduced in 2024 and features a quarter-pound flame-grilled beef patty, a maple bourbon BBQ sauce, crispy-fried onions, crispy-fried jalapenos, maple candied bacon, and American cheese, served on a toasted sesame seed bun. A Whopper Jr. version is also available.
Prices vary, but the Maple Bourbon BBQ Whopper costs $7.99 near me in Los Angeles, while the Maple Bourbon BBQ Whopper Jr. costs $4.79.
There's also a Maple BBQ Solo Moment meal and Family-Style BBQ Bundle as digital-exclusive specials when ordering through the Burger King website or mobile app. The Maple BBQ Solo Moment includes a Maple Bourbon BBQ Whopper, small French fries (or onion rings or Have-sies), a Cinnamon Apple Pie, and a small soda for $12 (may vary), while the Family-Style BBQ Bundles costs $27 and includes two Cheeseburgers, two Maple Bourbon BBQ Whoppers, four Cheesy Bacon Tots, two small orders of French fries, and two Cinnamon Apple Pie.
Photo via Burger King.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Maple Bourbon BBQ Whopper Returns to Burger King

articles.description:
Jack in the Box brings back the Hot Mess Cheeseburger for a limited time through April 5, 2026.

The Hot Mess Cheeseburger features a grilled beef patty, white cheese sauce, shredded pepper jack cheese, pickled jalapenos, and crispy-fried onion rings, served on toasted sourdough bread. A double version with two beef patties is also available. You can find my 2013 review of the Hot Mess Cheeseburger here.
There's also a Hot Mess Munchie Meal (pictured) that includes the burger plus two tacos, seasoned curly fries, a drink, and a limited-edition Meat Riot antenna ball (the Hot Mess was introduced back in 2013 with advertising featuring an '80s rock band by the name of Meat Riot).
The Hot Mess Cheeseburger starts at $5.99 (it's $6.99 here in Los Angeles), while the Hot Mess Munchie Meal starts at $13 ($14 near me).
Photo via Jack in the Box.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Jack in the Box Brings Back Hot Mess Cheeseburger

The Shamrock Shake features vanilla soft serve blended with minty (and green) Shamrock syrup, topped with whipped cream. The Oreo Shamrock McFlurry, on the other hand, features vanilla soft serve mixed with Shamrock syrup and Oreo cookie pieces.
Prices vary, but a location here in Los Angeles charges $4.39 for a medium Shamrock Shake and $4.59 for a regular-size Oreo Shamrock McFlurry.
Photo via McDonald's.

articles.title: Shamrock Shake and Oreo Shamrock McFlurry Return to McDonald's

articles.description:
Popeyes introduces new flavored Butterfly Shrimp and brings back its Flounder Fish Sandwich for the 2026 Lenten/seafood season.

While Popeyes' crispy-fried Butterfly Shrimp can be ordered in its original "Classic" flavor or in several flavors, including Garlic Parm, Lemon Pepper, Ghost Pepper, and Buffalo. They can be ordered as part of the Shrimp Tackle Box and Surf & Turf meal.
The Shrimp Tackle Box includes eight Butterfly Shrimp in your choice of flavor served with a regular side, a biscuit, and your choice of dipping sauce.
The Surf & Turf meal includes four Butterfly Shrimp and two Chicken Tenders served with a regular side, a biscuit, and your choice of dipping sauce.
Prices vary, but both the Shrimp Tackle Box and Surft & Turf meal start at $6.99 at a local Popeyes here in Los Angeles.
The Flounder Fish Sandwich features a crispy-fried Alaska flounder filet served with barrel-cured pickles and either Classic Tartar sauce or Spicy Spread on a toasted brioche bun. It costs $6.99 near me.
